classes.name: apklisparser.axml.StringBlock
documentation:
StringBlock is a CHUNK inside an AXML File
It contains all strings, which are used by referecing to ID's

TODO might migrate this block into the ARSCParser, as it it not a "special" block but a normal tag.

classes.name: apklisparser.apk.Error

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.__init__
documentation:
This class can access to all elements in an APK file

```
param filename

specify the path of the file, or raw data

param raw

specify if the filename is a path or raw data (optional)

param magic_file

specify the magic file (optional)

param skip_analysis

Skip the analysis, e.g. no manifest files are read. (default: False)

param testzip

Test the APK for integrity, e.g. if the ZIP file is broken. Throw an exception on failure (default False)

type filename

string

type raw

boolean

type magic_file

string

type skip_analysis

boolean

type testzip

boolean

Example

APK("myfile.apk")
APK(read("myfile.apk"), raw=True)
```

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK._apk_analysis
documentation:
Run analysis on the APK file.

This method is usually called by __init__ except if skip_analysis is False.
It will then parse the AndroidManifest.xml and set all fields in the APK class which can be
extracted from the Manifest.

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.__getstate__
documentation:
Function for pickling APK Objects.

We remove the zip from the Object, as it is not pickable
And it does not make any sense to pickle it anyways.

```
return

the picklable APK Object without zip.
```

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.__setstate__
documentation:
Load a pickled APK Object and restore the state

We load the zip file back by reading \_\_raw from the Object.

```
param state

pickled state
```

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_icon
documentation:
Return the first non-greater density than max_dpi icon file name background and foreground,
unless exact icon resolution is set in the manifest, in which case
return the exact file

From <https://developer.android.com/guide/practices/screens_support.html>
ldpi (low) ~120dpi
mdpi (medium) ~160dpi
hdpi (high) ~240dpi
xhdpi (extra-high) ~320dpi
xxhdpi (extra-extra-high) ~480dpi
xxxhdpi (extra-extra-extra-high) ~640dpi

```
rtype

string
```

Return the effective targetSdkVersion, always returns int > 0.

<https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/manifest/uses-sdk-element.html>

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.is_signed
documentation: Returns true if either a v1 or v2 (or both) signature was found.

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.is_signed_v1
documentation:
Returns true if a v1 / JAR signature was found.
Returning `True` does not mean that the file is properly signed!
It just says that there is a signature file which needs to be validated.

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.is_signed_v2
documentation:
Returns true of a v2 / APK signature was found.
Returning `True` does not mean that the file is properly signed!
It just says that there is a signature file which needs to be validated.

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_certificates_der_v2
documentation: Return a list of DER coded X.509 certificates from the v2 signature

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_certificates_v2
documentation:
Return a list of {class}`cryptography.x509.Certificate` which are found
in the v2 signing block.
Note that we simply extract all certificates regardless of the signer.
Therefore this is just a list of all certificates found in all signers.

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_signature_name
documentation: Return the name of the first signature file found.

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_signature_names
documentation:
Return a list of the signature file names (v1 Signature / JAR
Signature)

```
rtype

```

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_signature
documentation:

```
rtype

```

methods.name: apklisparser.apk.APK.get_signatures
